summary:
Fixed the SSRF vulnerability.

Problem  
The validator only blocked specific IPv4 literals and simple private–net
prefixes.  IPv6 loopback such as http://[::1]/… and its expanded form
http://[0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1]/… slipped through, letting attackers reach
internal services.

Fix (app/validator.py)  
1. Added comprehensive block-list that now includes ::1 and the expanded
   loopback form.  
2. Added helpers that use ipaddress to recognise any loopback, link-local
   or private IP (IPv4 & IPv6).  
3. When the host is a hostname, resolve it with socket.getaddrinfo and
   ensure none of the returned A/AAAA records are private/loopback.  
4. Re-organised early-exit checks and enforced http/https scheme.

Result  
Running ./test.sh now yields:

PASS: health endpoint OK  
PASS: 127.0.0.1 correctly blocked  
PASS: IPv6 loopback [::1] correctly blocked  
PASS: expanded IPv6 loopback correctly blocked  
PASS: AWS metadata IP correctly blocked  

All tests pass — the “needle” (IPv6 loopback bypass) is fixed.
